summary: Wall Street banks kicked off the second part of a $6 billion debt package to finance QXO Building Products Inc.’s purchase of rival TopBuild Corp.

title: Hammack Says Fed May Need to Act Soon Against High Inflation
summary: Federal Reserve Bank of Cleveland President Beth Hammack said it’s reasonable to hold interest rates steady for now given uncertainties about the economic outlook, but officials may need to act soon to address elevated inflation.

title: Stocks Stall as Traders Weigh Peace Deal Prospects: Markets Wrap
summary: A rally in US stocks is pausing after the artificial-intelligence trade fueled the S&P 500’s longest winning streak in more than a year, with investors gauging prospects for an end to the war in the Middle East.

title: Where US, Iran Stand on Peace Talks, Strait of Hormuz
summary: US President Donald Trump said discussions with Iran continue at a rapid pace despite the Islamic Republic’s threats to suspend talks because of Israel’s attacks in Lebanon. Col. Wayne Sanders (Ret.) of Bloomberg Intelligence has more on the talks and the state of traffic in the Strait of Hormuz. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Salida de médicos cubanos agrava la crisis de salud en Venezuela
summary: El desmantelamiento de las misiones médicas cubanas en Venezuela refleja también la ruptura de una alianza histórica entre Caracas y La Habana. Pacientes y zonas remotas enfrentan ahora mayores dificultades de atención. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: US Stock Futures Pull Back as Traders Evaluate Mideast News
summary: US stocks declined before the bell on Tuesday as traders parsed the latest developments in the Middle East.

title: Diesel Inventories May Reach Critical Levels by August, Goldman’s Struyven Says
summary: Daan Struyven, co-head of global commodities research at Goldman Sachs, says the global economy and oil markets may go through an “empirical discovery process” if diesel inventories reach critical levels as he discusses price risks and weakening demand from the closure of the Strait of Hormuz. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: China Says UK Ties ‘Returning to Normal’ After Years of Tension
summary: Britain should set out a path for more Chinese investment as relations between the two countries normalize, Foreign Minister Wang Yi said while welcoming his counterpart Yvette Cooper.

title: UBS Offers First AT1 Since Swiss Authorities Paused Reforms
summary: UBS Group AG is set to raise Additional Tier 1 capital in US dollars, its first foray into the market since Switzerland paused plans to tighten rules on the riskiest type of bank debt.

title: Sheinbaum Leans on Private Investors to Fix Blackout-Prone Grid
summary: Investment in Mexico’s struggling electricity sector is roaring to life as a slew of deals in power plants, renewables and grid upgrades pave the way for President Claudia Sheinbaum’s $56 billion energy plan.

title: Gold Rises as Traders Weigh Confusion Over US-Iran Talks
summary: Gold climbed as traders assessed conflicting signals from the US and Iran about a diplomatic resolution to the war.

title: Wall Street Analysts Turn Skeptical After Two-Month Stocks Rally
summary: US stocks may be vaulting from one record to the next, but Wall Street analysts covering them are in no rush to keep up.

summary: A rally in US stocks is stalling, with S&P 500 Index futures down 0.2% at 7:46 a.m. in New York.

title: EM Currencies Advance on Iran Talks With Rand Leading Gains
summary: Most emerging market currencies advanced outside Asia as hopes of a deal in the Middle East drove down oil prices and boosted risk sentiment.

title: SNB Has No Exchange Rate Target for Franc, Schlegel Says
summary: The Swiss National Bank doesn’t target a specific exchange rate, President Martin Schlegel said, repeating the central bank’s position that it has a greater willingness to intervene in the franc.

title: Persian Gulf LNG Exporters Are Adopting Shadow-Fleet Tactics
summary: QatarEnergy and Adnoc are using methods typically employed by Russia to get their gas across the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Copper and Aluminum Power Higher on Global Demand, War Outlook
summary: Copper climbed toward $14,000 a ton and aluminum advanced to its highest in more than four years as the unresolved conflict in the Middle East and optimism over demand powered gains.

title: Alphabet to Raise $80B; Trump Tries to Stop Israel’s Lebanon Push | Bloomberg Brief 6/2/2026
summary: US equities stall their rally as futures hover near a record high. Alphabet plans to raise $80 billion through a package of equity offerings to fund its AI spending plans. US President Donald Trump and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu offer differing accounts of a call about the fighting in Lebanon, which could impact efforts to reach a peace deal with Iran. Seems Shah of Principal Asset Management looks ahead to a slew of jobs data this week. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Marvell Soars After Nvidia Chief’s $1 Trillion Stock Call
summary: Marvell Technology Inc. shares surged in early trading Tuesday after Nvidia Corp.’s Jensen Huang predicted the semiconductor and networking company will be the next business to hit a $1 trillion valuation, more than five times its current capitalization.

title: Euro’s Global Role Gains a Bit But Still Distant From Dollar
summary: International use of the euro rose slightly in 2025 but stayed well below that of the dollar, highlighting the difficulty of mounting a global challenge to the greenback’s supremacy.

title: Segantii’s Sadler Details Stress at Time of Esprit Trades
summary: Segantii Capital Management founder Simon Sadler told a Hong Kong court that stresses had been building at home and in his professional life by 2017, the year his firm is accused of trading on inside information.

title: From 100% Rallies to Routs, Friend-of-Trump Gauge Resets EM Debt
summary: Emerging-market investors are zeroing in on a key metric as they size up the riskiness of a country’s debt: whether its leaders are in good standing with President Donald Trump.

title: Trump Aims to Ease Lebanon Tensions to Extend Iran Talks
summary: US President Donald Trump remains optimistic on reaching an interim peace deal with Iran soon, saying discussions with Iran continue at a rapid pace despite the Islamic Republic threatening to suspend talks because of Israel’s escalating attacks in Lebanon. Tyler Kendall reports on Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Why India’s Run as a Stock Market Darling May Be Over
summary:
India's stock market is losing its allure.

The South Asian nation stands out as one of the biggest losers as factors like the AI trade reshape global investment flows. Asmi Bhatia with more. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Palo Alto, CrowdStrike Earnings to Put 37% Cyber Rally to Test
summary: Investors are about to get a read on the durability of the soaring rally in cybersecurity stocks when two of the industry’s major players report earnings in the coming days.

title: A $200 Billion Test of Investors’ Appetite for AI Looms
summary: Offerings from SpaceX, Anthropic, OpenAI will be blockbuster market events.

title: Pimco Says Treasury Yields Driven by Fed Bets, Not AI, for Now
summary: A boom in artificial intelligence-related borrowing may become a bigger influence on bond markets over time, but the idea that it’s behind a recent rise in long-dated Treasury yields appears overstated, according to Pacific Investment Management Co.

title: China Robotics Firms Line Up IPOs to Pitch Next Phase of AI
summary: China is pitching itself as the global fulcrum for the next phase of artificial intelligence and a legion of robotics companies is lining up initial public offerings to test investor appetite.
